content_hints.description: Waterdeep's harbor in autumn. The smell of brine and roasting chestnuts mingles with the calliope music drifting from three gaudy ships moored at the end of the longest pier. Banners of crimson and gold snap in the harbor wind, advertising the Sea Maidens Faire -- a traveling carnival of exotic beasts, acrobats, and wonders from the Sword Coast. Dockhands avoid the pier. The carnival folk keep to themselves after dark.

npcs.name: Carnival Barker
npcs.dialogue: Step right up! The Sea Maidens Faire welcomes all! See the fire-eaters of Calimshan! Marvel at creatures from beyond the Trackless Sea! Captain Zord himself invites you aboard the Eyecatcher!
npcs.notes: Drow in disguise (hat of disguise). Reports all visitors to Jarlaxle. Will steer the party toward the Eyecatcher if they ask questions.

event_triggers: On entry: The carnival is winding down for the evening. Colored lanterns sway on lines between the three ships, and the last acrobats are taking their bows. A figure in a wide-brimmed hat watches from the Eyecatcher's quarterdeck.

content_hints.description: The main deck of the Eyecatcher, Jarlaxle's flagship. It is absurdly well-appointed for a carnival vessel -- the rails are polished mahogany, the brass fittings gleam, and the deck planks are oiled teak that do not creak underfoot. Caged animals line the port side: a displacer beast kitten chasing its own tentacles, a cockatrice in a gilded cage, a pseudodragon dozing on a velvet cushion. Everything is theatrical. Everything is a distraction.

npcs.name: Zardoz Zord
npcs.dialogue: Welcome aboard! I am Zardoz Zord, humble captain and purveyor of wonders. You look like the adventuring sort -- I can always tell. Something about the scars. Can I offer you Amnian wine? We have business to discuss, I think.
npcs.notes: Jarlaxle Baenre in magical disguise as a flamboyant human sea captain. Wearing a hat of disguise and ring of truth-telling (he knows if you lie). Charming, always smiling, always three steps ahead. Will not break cover unless forced.

event_triggers: On conversation (DC 18 Insight): Zord's accent slips for half a syllable -- something clipped and precise beneath the bravado. His eyes, for just a moment, are the wrong color.
On investigation (DC 20): The Eyecatcher rides too low in the water for a ship this size. Something heavy is underneath.

content_hints.description: The captain's quarters are a study in contradictions. Calishite tapestries hang beside Menzoberranzan spider-silk curtains. A desk of carved darkwood holds charts of Waterdeep's sewers alongside legitimate shipping manifests. A mirror on the wall is flanked by two rapiers -- one jeweled and decorative, the other plain and razor-sharp. The bed has not been slept in. A hidden panel in the floor leads below.

hazards.name: Glyph of Warding on desk drawer

event_triggers: On search (DC 15 Investigation): The shipping manifests reveal cargo deliveries to four noble houses -- all members of the Lords' Alliance.
On search (DC 18 Investigation): The hidden floor panel is found, revealing a ladder descending to the submarine dock.
content_hints.investigation_success: The manifests reveal a pattern of deliveries to Lords' Alliance members -- Jarlaxle is mapping their weaknesses.

content_hints.description: Below the main deck, hammocks swing from beams in tight rows. The crew here are lean, dark-eyed, and move with a dancer's grace that no human sailor possesses. Their belongings are sparse and identical -- each bunk has a sea chest containing a change of clothes, a hand crossbow wrapped in oilcloth, and a vial of something viscous and dark. In dim light, their skin has a faint purple undertone.
event_triggers: On combat: The drow fight in eerie silence, coordinating with hand signals. They aim to incapacitate, not kill -- Jarlaxle wants prisoners, not corpses.

content_hints.description: A circular shaft descends through the ship's keel into something that should not exist beneath a wooden hull. Metal rungs are set into a riveted iron tube that transitions from timber to steel halfway down. At the bottom, a pressurized hatch opens into a chamber lit by continual flame lanterns behind thick glass. The air tastes of machine oil and magic. You are now inside the Scarlet Marpenoth -- a submersible vessel.
event_triggers: On entry: The hatch seals behind you with a hiss of escaping air. A mechanical clicking begins somewhere in the walls -- the submarine is preparing to dive.

content_hints.description: The submarine's hold is divided into sealed compartments, each containing cargo too sensitive for the ships above. Crates stamped with the Bregan D'aerthe sigil hold weapons, disguise kits, forged documents of identity, and enough blackmail material to unseat half the Masked Lords. One compartment is climate-controlled and contains a single item on a velvet stand: a map of the Vault of Dragons with three possible entrances marked in red ink.

hazards.name: Alarm ward on Vault map case

event_triggers: On map read: The map reveals that Jarlaxle has already located the Vault of Dragons -- he just needs the dragonstaff of Ahghairon to open it.

content_hints.description: The submarine's navigation chamber. Brass instruments line the curved walls -- depth gauges, pressure dials, a compass that points to something other than north. A periscope extends through the hull. Through its eyepiece, you can see the harbor floor: silt, anchor chains, and the barnacled foundations of Waterdeep's piers. A speaking tube connects to the war room below, and through it you can hear a voice -- smooth, amused, speaking Elvish to someone who answers in Undercommon.

npcs.name: Fel'rekt Lafeen
npcs.dialogue: You should not be here. The captain does not appreciate uninvited guests in his private vessel. But he appreciates corpses even less -- they are terrible for business. So let us talk.
npcs.notes: Drow lieutenant of Bregan D'aerthe. Pragmatic and professional. Will negotiate if outmatched, fight if cornered. Knows the submarine's layout and Jarlaxle's schedule.

event_triggers: On negotiation: Fel'rekt offers a deal -- Jarlaxle will meet the party peacefully if they surrender their weapons. He keeps his word. Jarlaxle always keeps his word. The trick is what he does not say.

content_hints.description: The war room of the Scarlet Marpenoth is a drow mercenary's private sanctum rendered in mahogany and brass. Maps of a dozen cities cover the walls, each bristling with colored pins. A table of black glass reflects the continual flame lanterns like a dark mirror. And there, leaning against the table with a glass of wine, is Jarlaxle Baenre -- no longer Zardoz Zord, no longer disguised. Ebon skin, white hair cropped short on one side, an eye patch that is almost certainly magical, and a grin that promises he has been expecting you.
event_triggers: On entry: 'Ah, there you are. I was beginning to think you would never find the submarine. Most do not. Please, sit -- wine? No? Straight to business, then. I can respect that.' He does not draw a weapon. He does not need to.

On negotiation: Jarlaxle offers a deal: he wants the cache of dragons to buy Luskan a seat in the Lords' Alliance. He does not want war. He does not want blood. He wants legitimacy. 'Every lord in that Alliance got their seat through violence or inheritance. I merely propose a third option: commerce.'

On combat: Jarlaxle fights like a storm -- rapier in one hand, flying daggers from the bracer in the other, always moving, always grinning. If reduced below 50 HP he vanishes (Cape of Mountebank) and the submarine begins to flood. 3 rounds to escape.

On defeat: Jarlaxle surrenders with a bow. 'Well played. I concede this round. But I suspect we shall meet again -- I always enjoy a worthy adversary.' He means it. He does not suffer fools, but he respects skill.

content_hints.description: An emergency hatch at the submarine's stern opens into a flooded escape tube. Iron rungs lead up through ten feet of harbor water to a concealed platform beneath the Eyecatcher's hull. From there, a rope ladder reaches the pier above. The autumn air hits like a slap after the submarine's recycled atmosphere. Behind you, the Scarlet Marpenoth's running lights dim and the vessel sinks silently into the harbor mud.

hazards.name: Flooded escape tube (Constitution save or 1 level exhaustion)

event_triggers: On escape during flood: The submarine groans around you as seawater pours through breached seams. The escape tube is your only option -- swim or drown.
